errors.description: Tenant does not hold `transaction_capture` entitlement. Master feature gate.
Direct API creation of transactions is forbidden — transactions are recorded ONLY via TransactionRecorder from invoice / payment / refund / chargeback modules. Prevents ledger tampering.

tenant.transaction_halt_new=true — a P0 LedgerImbalanceDetected fired + halted new transactions until resolved. The originating recorder job is retryable — it will attempt again once ops clears the halt flag.
Cross-currency transaction attempted (currency != tenant.base_currency) but tenant lacks `transaction_multi_currency` entitlement. Medium+ tier.
Cross-currency transaction (currency != tenant.base_currency) missing exchange_rate_to_base OR base_amount_cents. Both must be set together.
base_amount_cents != ROUND(amount_cents * exchange_rate_to_base) within ±1 cent rounding tolerance. The caller computed base_amount_cents incorrectly.

The configured exchange-rate provider is unreachable + all fallbacks (ecb_daily) also failed. Cross-currency transaction refused. The originating recorder job is retryable — it will attempt again after the provider recovers.
The reference_type + reference_id pair does not resolve to an actual row OR the referenced entity belongs to a different tenant. Defense-in-depth against orphan transactions.
source_transaction_id set but reversal_reason missing. Reversal reason + source id travel as a pair.
reversal_reason set but source_transaction_id missing. Reversal reason + source id travel as a pair.
Attempted to reverse a transaction owned by a different tenant. Never valid — critical audit signal.
Reversal target is not in status='posted'. Reversals require the original be fully committed first.

Attempted to reverse a transaction that already has a reversal in this direction. A transaction can be reversed only once by a given reversal_reason (a refund-issued cannot be refunded twice). Repeated reversals require multiple different reversal_reason values or admin manual intervention.
The transaction.reverse feature flag is off. Ops set it during a regulator audit or a P0 incident.
Manual reversal via POST /transactions/{id}/reverse requires a written note ≥ 10 chars (configurable via transaction.reversal.min_note_length). Regulator-facing justification.
Attempted to UPDATE a posted transaction. Every posted transaction is IMMUTABLE. Corrections happen via new reversal transactions. Critical audit signal — should never fire in a healthy codepath.
Attempted to set posted_at outside the creating hook's atomic transaction. Never valid — the status transition draft→posted MUST happen inside the same DB transaction as the initial insert.
Attempted to attach a ledger entry to a transaction in a different tenant. Defense-in-depth against FK smuggling — critical audit signal.
Attempted to insert a new ledger entry on a transaction already in status='posted'. Entries attach ONLY during transaction creation.
Ledger entry account is not one of the 14 chart-of-accounts entries.
Ledger entry has both debit_cents > 0 AND credit_cents > 0, or both zero. Exactly one must be > 0.
Ledger entry account_currency does not match parent transaction currency. Single-currency-per-transaction invariant.
Attempted to UPDATE or DELETE a ledger entry. Every entry is IMMUTABLE. Critical audit signal — should never fire in a healthy codepath.
P0. SUM(debit_cents) != SUM(credit_cents) at transaction post time. Fires LedgerImbalanceDetected(scope='per_transaction') + halts the transaction. The caller must fix the entries before retrying.
Caller lacks the admin / finance role required for the transaction surface (viewAny/view). Reversal specifically requires admin (finance role does NOT have reverse permission).
Programmatic reconciliation via GET /api/v1/ledger/reconciliation requires the Enterprise-only transaction_ledger_reconciliation_api entitlement.
General Ledger report requires Medium+ transaction_advanced_reporting entitlement.
